name: Citrus Tomato Chicken
recipeInstructions:
MARINADE
2 Tablespoons apple juice
2 Tablespoons orange juice
1 Tablespoon lemon juice
1 Tablespoon balsamic vinegar
3 Pounds chicken pieces without skin
1 Pint apple juice
1 1/2 Cups chopped onions
1 Cup green bell pepper - chopped
1/2 Cup carrots - grated
1 Teaspoon garlic - minced
1/2 Pound canned tomato sauce
1 Cup fresh tomatoes
1 Cup chicken stock
1 Quart cooked rice
Combine the seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl.
Combine the marinade ingredients in a large bowl. Place the chicken in the bowl, pour the marinade over the chicken, cover, and refrigerate at least 4 hours, but preferably overnight.
Preheat the broiler. Remove the chicken from the marinade and reserve it. Place the chicken in a baking pan and broil until brown on both sides, about 7 minutes total. Remove the chicken and set aside.
While the chicken is broiling, reduce the apple juice to 1 cup.
Preheat a heavy 5-quart pot over high heat to 350°, about 4 minutes.
Add the onions, bell peppers, carrots, and 1 tablespoon of the seasoning mix; cover, and cook for 3 minutes. At this point, the vegetables will start to stick and turn light brown.
Add the garlic, stir well, and scrape the bottom of the pot to clear it of all brown bits. Cover and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
Add the reduced apple juice and remaining seasoning mix; cover, and scrape the bottom clear. The mixture will have turned medium brown by now. Cook for 4 minutes.
Stir in the tomato sauce, diced tomatoes, stock, and reserved marinade. Add the chicken to the pot, bring to a boil, reduce the heat to medium, and cook until the meat is tender, about 20 minutes.
Serve over rice.
